payload.answer: Recall selected mem_new because it is the current memory for Sam from Goldman. mem_old was also related to the query, but it was excluded from the active result set because its supersession metadata indicates that mem_new replaced it. Therefore mem_old should only be referenced as a filtered, superseded record, not as a current fact.
payload.decision: Recall returned mem_new because it matched the query for Sam from Goldman and is the current, non-superseded memory. mem_old matched the same subject/context but was filtered out because it is marked as superseded by mem_new.

entity_resolution.reason: The query phrase identified Sam with Goldman as contextual disambiguation. Punctuation differences did not justify treating this as a different entity or memory target.

memory_cards.statement: mem_new is the active recall result for the query about Sam from Goldman; mem_old is not current because it has been superseded.

details: Explained why mem_old was filtered as superseded.
Did not present mem_old as a current fact.

repair_options: No repair needed if mem_new is the intended replacement for mem_old.
If mem_old was incorrectly marked superseded, remove or correct the supersession link.
If Sam from Goldman was resolved to the wrong entity, adjust entity resolution or add a clearer disambiguating memory.
